title: ViewSonic VX2730D-4K 27-inch 4K dual-refresh gaming monitor review: Delivering speed, color, accuracy, and pixel density
summary: The ViewSonic VX2730D-4K 27-inch 4K gaming monitor offers high performance and accurate color at an affordable price.
insight: Balanced performance meets affordability in a 4K gaming monitor.

title: Intel hires former SK hynix chief Seok-Hee Lee to lead Intel Foundry advanced packaging company establishing section as 'focused business with dedicated leadership'
summary: Intel appoints Seok-Hee Lee as EVP of Intel Foundry, bringing experience from SK hynix and SK On. Lee will lead the advanced packaging company, establishing it as a 'focused business with dedicated leadership'. This move aims to enhance Intel's foundry capabilities.
insight: Intel seeks industry expertise to boost foundry business and competitiveness.

title: CEO of Chinese Anthropic rival tells Elon Musk that China will have a Fable 5-class AI model before next year it won t take that long says Jie Tang in response to Musk's prediction of a Q1 target
summary: Elon Musk predicts Chinese AI firms will reach Mythos level by Q1 2027, but Z.ai CEO Jie Tang believes they will achieve this soon, without specifying a timeline.
insight: China's AI advancements challenge Musk's predictions and global technology leadership

title: SK Telecom named as the Korean carrier at the center of Anthropic's Mythos export controls controversy access was revoked days before White House took Mythos and Fable 5 offline for all foreign nationals
summary: SK Telecom's access to Anthropic's Mythos model was revoked by the White House, citing alleged ties to China. This decision came days before the White House took Mythos and Fable 5 offline for foreign nationals. The move aims to prevent potential export control breaches.
insight: Export controls protect sensitive AI tech from falling into adversarial hands abroad.

title: 16-year-old SATA II SSD survives 1 petabyte of writes 25x more than the drive's endurance rating
summary: A 16-year-old SATA II SSD has withstood 1 petabyte of writes, exceeding its endurance rating.
insight: Legacy SSDs can still perform beyond expected lifespan capacities.

title: Tesco UK supermarket chain removes 40,000 servers from VMware infrastructure mass exodus continues due to Broadcom's aggressive subscription model
summary: Tesco UK has migrated 40,000 servers away from VMware's infrastructure due to Broadcom's aggressive subscription model. This move is part of a larger exodus from VMware, driven by concerns over the company's pricing strategy. The shift aims to reduce costs and improve efficiency.
insight: Broadcom's pricing model sparks mass VMware exodus among major enterprises

title: US energy regulator to order grid operators to expedite AI data center applications says projects should bring their own power or cut usage during high demand
summary: FERC orders grid operators to expedite AI data center connections that generate their own power or reduce peak demand within 90 days.
insight: AI data centers must adapt to grid capacity or power themselves.

title: Scammers in China sell $222 RTX 4090 with fake GPU die made out of plastic instead of real silicon marked with 2030 production dates, the card didn't even have working VRAM
summary: Scammers in China are selling fake RTX 4090 GPUs with plastic dies, deceiving buyers with 2030 production dates and non-functional VRAM.
insight: Counterfeit GPU technology poses significant risks to consumer trust and market security.

title: Best USB Chargers 2026: Our tested phone and laptop charger picks, from compact GaN to budget charging bliss
summary: We tested 20 laptop and phone chargers, identifying top performers from affordable to high-end options.
insight: Efficient chargers can significantly reduce energy waste and environmental impact.

title: MSI's new Claw 8 EX AI+ handheld with an Intel Arc G3 Extreme and 32GB of RAM costs $1,799 company says it'll be 'a tough year' with chances of 'another price hike'
summary: MSI's Claw 8 EX AI+ handheld costs $1,799 with Intel Arc G3 Extreme and 32GB RAM. The company anticipates a tough year with potential price hikes. This may deter potential buyers.
insight: High-end gaming handhelds come with hefty price tags, unpredictable costs.

title: Ditching the cloud for local AI how I use two mini PCs to process millions of tokens a day and save money on costly API fees
summary: I built a local AI infrastructure using mini PCs to process millions of tokens daily, reducing costly API fees. This setup allows for more control and cost-effectiveness in AI model deployment. By leveraging local processing power, I can save money and improve efficiency.
insight: Rolling your own AI models can save you thousands in API fees annually.

title: Noctua NL-LC1-36 Review: Compromise paves the way
summary: Noctua's NL-LC1-36 AIO cooler is a promising alternative to air cooling, offering improved pump performance. The product is a departure from Noctua's traditional air coolers and fans, but maintains their brand reputation for quality. AIO liquid coolers are an attractive option for users seeking a hassle-free cooling solution.
insight: Pump performance redefines AIO coolers with improved efficiency potential.

title: Retro pirate gets two-year suspended jail sentence for being stuck in the past, burning and selling remix CDs of famous artists four-year investigation into copyright infringement on 40-year-old medium began in 2018
summary: A UK man was sentenced for breaking copyright laws by selling unauthorized music mix CDs. The case began in 2018 and involved remixes of famous artists. The man received a two-year suspended jail sentence.
insight: Copyright laws protect artists' rights beyond physical medium obsolescence.

title: CyberPowerPC Gaming Desktop (GXi3800BSTV2) review: A showpiece with real muscle
summary: The CyberPowerPC Gaming Desktop (GXi3800BSTV2) is a stylish and powerful gaming PC that combines style and speed at an affordable price.
insight: Aesthetic appeal meets exceptional gaming performance under budget.

title: Bambu Lab launches PLA Pure filament New material boasts kid-safe toy certifications and "asbestos-free" talc
summary: Bambu Lab introduces PLA Pure filament with kid-safe toy certifications and asbestos-free talc, a departure from traditional PLA.
insight: New PLA material prioritizes safety over traditional composition.

title: Apple CEO Tim Cook warns AI-driven price increases are unavoidable says company is trying its best but 'the situation has become unsustainable'
summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook warns of unavoidable price increases due to DRAM and NAND memory costs.
insight: DRAM and NAND memory shortages cause unsustainable price hikes.

title: Rotary Mouse puts a rotary wheel into a standard desktop mouse (Crowdfunding)
summary: Airra Labs introduces the Rotary Mouse, a standard desktop mouse with a rotary wheel for smoother scrolling. The device comes in two variants: standard and vertical. This allows for faster and more comfortable scrolling during long sessions.
insight: Revolutionizes mouse scrolling with a more ergonomic circular motion.

title: Matter 1.6 specification adds NFC-based commissioning, thermostat suggestions, various core enhancements
summary: The Matter 1.6 specification adds NFC-based commissioning and Thermostat Suggestions, enhancing device communication and security.
insight: Simplifies smart device setup with NFC-based commissioning functionality.

title: Qualcomm promises a major reset with upstream-first, Qualcomm Linux 2.0 for Dragonwing IoT platforms
summary: Qualcomm is shifting to an upstream-first, open development model with Qualcomm Linux 2.0, aiming to reduce friction and make builds more predictable across Dragonwing IoT platforms.
insight: Unified open development model for improved IoT platform consistency

title: UP WCL A credit card-sized Wildcat Lake SBC with up to 24GB LPDDR5, 256GB UFS
summary: The AAEON UP WCL is a compact SBC featuring Intel Wildcat Lake processors with up to 24GB LPDDR5 and 256GB UFS storage, offering various I/O options for expansion.
insight: High-performance computing in a credit card-sized, compact form factor emerges.

title: Midjourney wants to scan your body with half a million ultrasonic sensors, at a spa
summary: Midjourney proposes a novel system using 500,000 ultrasonic sensors for non-invasive scanning.
insight: Accurate, real-time 3D body mapping for health and wellness applications.

title: The Real Reason Nvidia Has Abandoned PC Gamers
summary: Nvidia's shift away from PC gaming is driven by slowing GPU gains, rising manufacturing costs, and longer upgrade cycles. This makes PC gamers a less attractive business segment. As a result, Nvidia is focusing on more profitable areas.
insight: Gaming revenue is no longer driving Nvidia's growth trajectory.

title: Old-school crime: man gets suspended jail sentence for burning and selling pirated CDs
summary: A 47-year-old man from East Yorkshire was given a suspended jail sentence for burning and selling pirated CDs after a two-year investigation.
insight: Intellectual property theft can lead to serious consequences years later.

title: The first unpatchable iPhone exploit in six years targets chips still running Apple's latest iOS
summary: Security researchers at Paradigm Shift have published the first iPhone bootROM exploit in years, targeting a hardware-level flaw that can only be fixed by upgrading to newer hardware.
insight: Hardware-level vulnerability makes iOS upgrades ineffective against bootROM exploits.

title: AMD resolves HandBrake scaling issues on Threadripper, gains reach 215%
summary: AMD resolves HandBrake scaling issues on Threadripper with significant performance gains.
insight: Optimized scaling on high-core-count CPUs boosts transcoding speeds dramatically.

title: Frontier Airlines is leaking your passport and credit card details from a boarding pass
summary: Frontier Airlines boarding passes contain sensitive personal data, potentially exposing passengers to identity theft.
insight: Exposure of passport and credit card details compromises passenger security permanently online.

title: Minecraft's Chaos Cubed update adds Sulfur Caves and the unpredictable Sulfur Cube
summary: Minecraft Java Edition 26.2 introduces Sulfur Caves, Sulfur Cube mob, and Friends List system.
insight: New Sulfur Cube mob expands gameplay possibilities and interactions.

title: Microsoft and Adobe team up and make Photoshop 20% faster on Windows
summary: Microsoft engineers have improved Photoshop performance on Windows through a collaboration with Adobe, focusing on the Visual C++ compiler to enhance native application speed.
insight: Boosting Photoshop speed on Windows can benefit creative professionals worldwide.

title: Cybercriminals have been distributing malware via Steam for a year, tens of thousands affected
summary: Malware was distributed via Steam's Wallpaper Engine for over a year, affecting tens of thousands of users. The attack exploited Wallpaper Engine's unverified third-party code execution feature. This allowed threat actors to target user systems.
insight: Unverified code execution in popular apps can enable large-scale cyberattacks.

title: Sandisk's new PS5 SSDs cost up to $2,960, that's five PlayStation 5 consoles
summary: Sandisk has launched PS5 SSDs priced up to $2,960, exceeding console costs.
insight: Expensive PS5 SSDs hinder console upgrade affordability and flexibility.

title: Apple's iPhone Air 2 is getting a second camera and better battery life, coming next year
summary: The next iPhone Air is reportedly getting a second camera and improved battery life.
insight: Enhanced camera capabilities boost appeal to photography enthusiasts.

title: Taiwan is teaching civilians drone skills learned from Ukraine's war
summary: Taiwan is empowering civilians with drone skills for defense against China.
insight: Civilian-led defense efforts bolster Taiwan's resilience against potential invasion threats.

title: Just 16% of Americans think AI will benefit society, despite chatbot use climbing to 49% of US adults
summary: 49% of US adults use chatbots, up from 33% two years ago, but 51% don't use them at all, with the majority being 50 and older. Despite increasing use, most Americans are skeptical about AI's benefits to society.
insight: Growing chatbot adoption contrasts with public skepticism of AI benefits.

title: AMD confirms next-gen Threadripper with Zen 6 cores, PCIe 6.0, and new TR6 socket
summary: AMD confirms the next-gen Threadripper with Zen 6 cores, PCIe 6.0, and DDR5 memory.
insight: Upgrade to Zen 6 cores offers significant performance boost for high-end users.
